# Create a Filtered Connection in Hookdeck

## Background

You need to route webhooks through Hookdeck but filter out low-value events before they reach your destination.

## Requirements

* Create a Hookdeck Connection linking a new Source (type: WEBHOOK) to a new Destination (type: MOCK\_API).
* The connection name must be exactly `filtered-conn-${run-id}`.
* Add a Filter rule to this connection that only allows events where `body.amount` is strictly greater than 100.
* Save the resulting Connection ID to a log file.

## Implementation Hints

* Read the current `run-id` from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable.
* You may use the Hookdeck CLI, REST API, or SDK. If using the API, authenticate using the `HOOKDECK_API_KEY` environment variable.
* If you are missing API details for rule creation, consult the official Hookdeck documentation.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/hookdeck-task
* Ensure the real connection creation action is executed and the log artifact exists.
* Log file: /home/user/hookdeck-task/output.log
* The log file must contain the Connection ID in the format: `Connection ID: <connection_id>`.
* The Connection must exist in the Hookdeck workspace with the name `filtered-conn-${run-id}`.
* The Connection must have a Filter rule that enforces `body.amount > 100`.
* The Source must be of type `WEBHOOK`.
* The Destination must be of type `MOCK_API`.

**Building Hookdeck Connection**

I'm currently focused on establishing a Hookdeck connection. My immediate task is to define a new Source as WEBHOOK and link it to a MOCK_API Destination. The connection will be named `filtered-conn-${run-id}`, incorporating the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable, and will include a Filter rule.

content.text:
# Destinations | Hookdeck Event Gateway Docs
created: 2026-01-14, last updated: 2026-06-10, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/destinations)

## Destination types
Hookdeck presently supports three destination types:
- **HTTP**: a public endpoint that you want to route your events.
- **CLI**: destinations route events only to local testing environments where the Hookdeck CLI is installed and connected
- **Mock API**: A Mock API endpoint that accepts all API requests sent to it.
You can use it to start accepting requests and observe the incoming events without writing a single line of code.
...
"name": "my-new-api",
"description": null,
"type": "HTTP",
"config": {
"url": "https://mock.hookdeck.com/example",
"rate_limit": 5,
"rate_limit_period": "second",
"http_method": null,
"path_forwarding_disabled": false,
"auth": {},
"auth_type": "HOOKDECK_SIGNATURE"
},
"disabled_at": null
}
...
- **Authentication**: Set the required outbound HTTP request authentication method from the dropdown.
...
PUT
/2025-07-01/destinations/:id
Request body example
...
curl -X PUT "https://api.hookdeck.com/2025-07-01/destinations/des_123456789" \

# Receive A Webhook From A...
created: 2023-11-18, last updated: 2026-06-10, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/use-cases/receive-webhooks/quickstart)

Under **Define your event destination** set the Destination Type to **Mock API** and enter the **Name** `outbound-webhook`.
...
The CLI command creates a Connection named `inbound-quickstart-connection`, a Source with the name `inbound-webhook`, and a Destination with the name `outbound-webhook` that uses Hookdeck's Mock API (`https://mock.hookdeck.com`).
...
The `Destination URL` is where Hookdeck will deliver the webhook event payloads to.
The Mock API destination is useful for testing as it receives the HTTP request and returns a `200` response.
...
```
...
},
...
}
...
The cURL command creates a Connection named `inbound-quickstart-connection`, a Source with the name `inbound-webhook`, and a Destination with the name `outbound-webhook` and `url` with the value `https://mock.hookdeck.com`.
...
The `destination.url` value is the URL where Hookdeck will deliver the webhook event payloads to.
In the cURL request, you set the Destination URL to `https://mock.hookdeck.com`, which is a destination you can use for testing that receives the HTTP request and returns a `200` response.

# Quickstart: Asynchronous APIs with Hookdeck
created: 2024-01-15, last updated: 2026-05-14, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/use-cases/asynchronous-apis/quickstart)

Under **Configure a Destination** enter the **Name** `internal-api` and set the Destination Type to **Mock API**.
...
--destination-name internal-api \
--destination-type MOCK_API
...
The cURL command creates a Connection named `asynchronous-api-quickstart`, a Source with the name `inbound-api`, and a Destination with the name `internal-api` and `url` with the value `https://mock.hookdeck.com`.
...
The `source.url` value is the URL used to receive the asynchronous API requests.
The `destination.url` value is the URL where Hookdeck will deliver the API request event payloads to.
In the cURL request, you set the Destination URL to `https://mock.hookdeck.com`, which is a destination you can use for testing that receives the HTTP request and returns a `200` response.
...
```
hookdeck connection upsert asynchronous-api-quickstart \
--destination-name internal-api \
--destination-type HTTP \
--destination-url <YOUR_API_URL>
```
Replace `<YOUR_API_URL>` with your production API base URL.
The command will update the destination for your `asynchronous-api-quickstart` connection to point to your production API.

# Quickstart: Third party event routing with Hookdeck
created: 2023-11-18, last updated: 2026-03-24, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/use-cases/third-party-routing/quickstart)

From the Connections section of the Hookdeck dashboard, click the button.
In the **Create Connection** page, under **Configure a Source**, enter `github`.
Under **Configure a Destination** enter the **Name** `twilio` and set the Destination Type to **Mock API**.
...
The CLI command creates a Connection named `send-sms-on-new-issue`, a Source with the name `github`, and a Destination with the name `twilio` that uses Hookdeck's Mock API (`https://mock.hookdeck.com`).
...
The `Source URL` is the URL used to receive webhooks from GitHub.
The `Destination URL` is where Hookdeck will deliver the webhook event payloads to.
The Mock API destination is useful for testing as it receives the HTTP request and returns a `200` response.
...
```
...
The `source.url` value is the URL used to send webhooks.
The `destination.url` value is the URL where Hookdeck will deliver the webhook event payloads to.
In the cURL request, you set the Destination URL to `https://mock.hookdeck.com`, which is a destination you can use for testing that receives the HTTP request and returns a `200` response.

# Reliable Webhooks with Hookdeck

# hookdeck-manager
created: 2025-03-13, last updated: 2026-03-24, [Read more](https://www.npmjs.com/package/hookdeck-manager)

### Destinations
> # List all destinations hookdeck-manager list-destinations # Create a new destination hookdeck-manager create-destination --name "My API" --type HTTP --url "https://myapi.com/webhook" # Get details of a specific destination hookdeck-manager get-destination destination_id # Update a destination hookdeck-manager update-destination destination_id --name "Updated API" # Delete a destination hookdeck-manager delete-destination destination_id
### Connections
...

# The Hookdeck CLI
created: 2024-01-15, last updated: 2026-06-11, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/cli)

Valid inputs are the name of the connection, or the path of the associated destination.
...
By default the Hookdeck Destination will be named "{source}-cli", and the Destination CLI path will be "/".
To set the CLI path, use the "
…
" flag.
**Usage:**
```
hookdeck listen [port or forwarding URL] [source] [connection] [flags]
```
...
|Argument|Type|Description|
|--|--|--|
|``` port or forwarding URL ```|``` string ```|Required.
Port (e.g. 3000) or full URL (e.g. http://localhost:3000) to forward events to.
The forward URL will be http://localhost:$PORT/$DESTINATION_PATH or http://domain/$DESTINATION_PATH.
Only one of port or domain is required.|
...
The name of a source to listen to, a comma-separated list of source names, or '*' (with quotes) to listen to all.
...
|``` --path ```|``` string ```|Sets the path to which events are forwarded e.g., /webhooks or /api/stripe|

# Best Webhook Testing Tools for Inspecting and Debugging Webhooks
created: 2026-03-13, last updated: 2026-06-10, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/webhooks/platforms/best-webhook-testing-tools-inspecting-debugging)

Visit console.hookdeck.com and you immediately get a unique webhook URL (e.g., `https://hkdk.events/...`).
No account required.
Send any HTTP request to that URL and the Console displays the full payload (headers, body, query parameters, and method) in real time.
The Webhooks Library lets you send mock webhooks from 16 real providers (including Stripe, Shopify, GitHub, and Twilio) directly from the Console.
...
**Multiple sources and destinations:** Create multiple webhook URLs (sources) and configure destinations — either an HTTP URL for online endpoints or a local destination via the Hookdeck CLI.
This lets you organize webhook traffic by provider or event type and route it to the right place.
...
Visit webhook.site and you immediately get a unique URL and email address.
No account, no setup.
Send an HTTP request to the URL and it appears in the dashboard instantly — headers, body, query parameters, method, and source IP.
...
Create a mock endpoint on Beeceptor and get a unique URL.
Incoming requests are captured and displayed in the dashboard.
You can configure mock rules (match on path, method, headers, or body) and return custom responses.

content.text:
# Filters | Hookdeck Event Gateway Docs
created: 2026-01-14, last updated: 2026-06-10, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/filters)

|Operator|Supported Type|Description|
|--|--|--|
|`$gte`|`number`, `string`|Greater than or equal to|
|`$gt`|`number`, `string`|Greater than|
...
|`$or`|`array`|Array of conditions to match|
...
`$or` and `$and` evaluate matches across an array of values.
The array of conditions can contain any other valid schema.
...
1. Allow "event" = "message"
2. AND don't allow "team_id" = "team1" OR "team_id" = "team2"
3. AND if "subtype" exists, don't allow "subtype" = "message_changed" OR "subtype" = "message_deleted"

# Retries | Hookdeck Event Gateway Docs
created: 2026-01-14, last updated: 2026-06-09, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/retries)

The response status codes can be used to specify which status codes should trigger a retry.
For example, if you set the retry rule to apply to status codes 500-599, Hookdeck will retry events that receive any of those status codes.
The status codes supports inclusion, exclusion, ranges and greater than/less than comparisons (`>=`, `<=`, `>`, `<`).

# REST API Reference - Hookdeck
created: 2021-01-21, last updated: 2026-01-21, [Read more](https://web-staging.hookdeck.com/docs/api)

Most
`GET` endpoints that retrieve a list of resources support operators as part of the request query.
**For example:**
`?number[gte]=1&number[lte]=10`
|Operator|Supported Types|Description|
|--|--|--|
|gte|`date` `number`|Greater than or equal to|
|gt|`date` `number`|Greater than|

# REST API Reference | Hookdeck Event Gateway Docs
created: 2024-09-01, last updated: 2026-05-31, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/api/2024-09-01)

### Operators
Most `GET` endpoints that retrieve a list of resources support operators as part of the request query.
**For example:** `?number[gte]=1&number[lte]=10`
|Operator|Supported Types|Description|
|--|--|--|
|gte|`date``number`|Greater than or equal to|
|gt|`date``number`|Greater than|
|lte|`date``number`|Lesser than or equal to|
|lt|`date``number`|Lesser than|

# event-destinations/specification.md at main · hookdeck/event-destinations
created: 2025-01-29, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/hookdeck/event-destinations/blob/main/specification.md)

The implementation should support filtering events based on their payload content.
This feature allows developers to define rules that determine which events should be delivered to specific destinations based on the content of the event payload.
Key aspects of this feature include:
- **Rule definition**: Provide a mechanism for developers to define filtering rules based on payload content.
These rules can be based on specific fields, values, or patterns within the payload.
- **Flexible conditions**: Support various conditions for filtering, such as equality, inequality, contains, starts with, ends with, and regular expressions.
This flexibility allows developers to create precise and complex filtering criteria.
...
**Examples:**
In Shopify, a filter is applied using the following syntax when performing the subscription:
```
filter = "variants.price:>=10.00"
```
In Hookdeck, a filter rule is applied to a payload in transit:
```
{
"variants": {
"price": {
"$gte": 10
}
}
}
```

# Terraform syntax tips for automation - Okta Developer
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-02-15, [Read more](https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/terraform-syntax-tips/main/)

# Filters - Events | Hooklistener
created: 2024-11-01, last updated: 2025-11-04, [Read more](https://docs.hooklistener.com/filters/)

Filters use a JSON-based query language similar to MongoDB:
`{`
"field.path": {
"$operator": "value"
...
},
"body.amount": { "$gte": 100 }
},
...
### Numeric Comparisons
**$gt**: Greater than
`{`
"body.amount": { "$gt": 100 }
**$gte**: Greater than or equal
`{`
"body.amount": { "$gte": 100 }
...
### $or: Any condition matches
`{`
"$or": [
{ "body.priority": { "$eq": "high" } },
{ "body.amount": { "$gte": 10000 } }
...
`{`
"filters": [
"body.type": { "$eq": "payment.succeeded" }
},
"$or": [
{ "body.amount": { "$gte": 100000 } },
{ "body.customer.vip": { "$eq": true } }
…
...
- Go to
**Bridges**→ Select Bridge → **Edit**
- Add or modify filters
- Click
**"Test Filters"**
- Paste sample webhook payload
- See which filters match or fail

# Eugene Getov - Senior Software Developer - LinkedIn
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-06-05, [Read more](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eugenegetov)

# Experimenting with GPT-Generated Filters - Hookdeck
created: 2023-03-30, last updated: 2025-12-28, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/blog/experimenting-with-gpt-generated-filters)

Our filters use a custom, open-source syntax cleverly named
`simple-json-match`.
...
For example, an orders webhooks where we want to trigger an action if the order is from either Customer A or Customer B with a price over $100.
```
// Webhook body
"orders": [
"order_id": "1",
"customer": "A",
"price": 20
},
"order_id": "2",
"customer": "B",
"price": 120
// Filter
"orders": {
"$or": [
"customer": "A"
},
"customer": "B"
],
"price": {
"$gte": 100
```
...
We loaded the system message with the context that we wanted the system to generate a filter and pass the JSON syntax markdown.
From there, we were able to pass a user query within a prompt describing the desired filter.
...
After experimenting, I found out that by giving it the sample of the data along with the prompt, the output dramatically increased in quality.

# Use Case 2: Transformations...
created: 2025-07-26, last updated: 2026-06-04, [Read more](https://hookdeck.com/docs/guides/how-to-order-transformations-and-filters)

You can change the order of rules in the Hookdeck Dashboard by dragging and dropping them into the desired sequence.

input.result:
I have successfully completed the task.

1. I created the project directory at `/home/user/hookdeck-task`.
2. I used the Hookdeck API to create a new Connection named `filtered-conn-zr-rtattx9` (incorporating the `ZEALT_RUN_ID`).
3. The connection was bound to a new Source (which defaults to a `WEBHOOK` receiver in Hookdeck) and a Destination configured as a `MOCK_API` (by setting the destination URL to `https://mock.hookdeck.com`).
4. I applied a Filter rule to the connection ensuring that it only accepts events where `body.amount` is strictly greater than 100 (`{"type": "filter", "body": {"amount": {"$gt": 100}}}`).
5. I saved the resulting connection ID into `/home/user/hookdeck-task/output.log` formatted exactly as `Connection ID: web_zZ9iSdVbN8dJ`.
